name: listen
description: Reachable address and port to listen on for incoming client connections and RPCs from other nodes.

name: peers
description: Comma-separated list of peer Cores in an existing cluster. If non-empty, the Core attempts to join the cluster by connecting to each peer; otherwise it bootstraps a new cluster when no existing data is found.

name: data
description: Directory where the Core will store its data.

name: mem
description: Use an in-memory data store. Data will be lost on restart; not recommended for production.

name: insecure
description: Disable TLS encryption, authentication, and authorization. Not recommended for production.

name: auto-cert
description: Automatically generate self-signed certificates for the Core.

name: no-driver
description: Disable the embedded Synnax Driver. Useful when running the Core without any device integration.

name: slow-consumer-timeout
description: Duration after which a telemetry streamer is closed if it stops consuming messages from the relay. Rarely needs adjustment.

name: enable-integrations
description: Device integrations to enable (arc, ethercat, labjack, modbus, ni, opc). All integrations are enabled by default.

name: disable-integrations
description: Device integrations to disable (arc, ethercat, labjack, modbus, ni, opc). All integrations are enabled by default.

name: task-op-timeout
description: Duration before reporting stuck task operations in the embedded Driver. Useful for debugging tasks that may be blocking.

name: task-poll-interval
description: Interval between task timeout checks in the embedded Driver.

name: task-shutdown-timeout
description: Maximum time to wait for task workers during embedded Driver shutdown. Stuck workers are detached after this timeout.

name: task-worker-count
description: Number of worker threads for task operations in the embedded Driver. Valid range is 1-64.

name: disable-channel-name-validation
description: Allow channels to be created with any name, including special characters and spaces. By default, channel names must contain only letters, digits, and underscores, and may not start with a digit.
